events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate scenario: crew proposes shortcut for conductor selection & sizing, submittal omits product listing page and project note that would decide whether shortcut allowed. Reference NEC 310, Table 310.16. Stage in-progress. Need decision pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since shortcut may be allowed but missing documentation to decide, practical compliant course: stop work / fail until documentation provided? The instruction: If work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Is it non-compliant? Proposing a shortcut without verifying listing/project note is not yet installed maybe. In-progress, no components flagged. Need choose practical compliant course. Since submittal omits product listing page and project note that would decide whether shortcut allowed, cannot approve. Decision likely needs_more_info because missing info. But if dangerous? Not necessarily dangerous yet. However task says choose practical, compliant course. A shortcut for conductor selection & sizing without verifying product listing and project note is a fail because it violates NEC 110.3(B) installation per listing and 310 conductor selection. But decision options include needs_more_info. The scenario: "submittal omits the product listing page and project note that would decide whether the shortcut is allowed here." That implies need more info. But if crew proposes shortcut and can't prove allowed, inspector would fail/reject. Required: If work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Is proposed shortcut non-compliant? It is non-compliant to take a shortcut not verified as allowed. But maybe it's just incomplete submittal, not yet installed. In-progress actively. We can say fail due to incomplete submittal / unverified shortcut; work cannot proceed until listing and project note provided. Or needs_more_info. Let's parse wording: "The crew proposes a shortcut ... but the submittal omits the product listing page and project note that would decide whether the shortcut is allowed here." We are to evaluate field condition and return decision. Since missing documentation, the appropriate decision is needs_more_info? But the instruction says if dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. The shortcut itself could be non-compliant because conductor selection must be based on NEC 310.16 and product listing; without listing, cannot confirm ampacity/temperature rating/termination compatibility. A proposed unverified shortcut is a code violation risk. In inspection context, you'd fail it and require documentation. However "needs_more_info" is also valid when insufficient evidence. The question asks "choose the practical, compliant course of action given the field constraints". Practical course: do not allow shortcut; require product listing page and project note; size per NEC 310/Table 310.16. That is a fail of the proposed shortcut, with actions. But decision could be "fail" because the proposed work is non-compliant (shortcut not substantiated). Let's decide fail.

Need fill JSON fields. component_conditions: none flagged, empty list. work_stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 40? value not asked 0. sound_source etc default empty string/0. order empty. workable empty. findings: shortcut proposed without required listing/project note; cannot verify conductor ampacity, temperature rating, termination compatibility; potential NEC 110.3(B) and 310 violation. actions: reject shortcut; obtain product listing page and project note; verify conductor sizing per NEC 310.16 and termination ratings; resubmit for review; do not install until approved. rationale: NEC requires conductors selected/sized per ampacity tables and installed per listing/labeling; missing documentation prevents verifying shortcut, so non-compliant/prohibited until substantiated. references: NEC 110.3(B), NEC 310, Table 310.16. risk high? Potential fire/overheating if undersized; high. confidence 0.9. event_time 0. rate 0. sound_source "".…

  "findings": [
    "Crew proposes a conductor selection/sizing shortcut without the product listing page or project note needed to approve it.",
    "Cannot verify conductor ampacity, temperature rating, or termination compatibility for the proposed shortcut.",
    "Unverified shortcut risks violating NEC conductor sizing and use-per-listing requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the proposed shortcut.",
    "Require the missing product listing page and project note before any further work.",
    "Verify final conductor sizing per NEC 310 and Table 310.16, including termination ratings and conditions of use.",
    "Resubmit the corrected conductor selection/sizing package for review prior to installation."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 110.3(B) requires equipment to be installed and used per its listing, and NEC 310/Table 310.16 governs conductor ampacity selection. Without the listing documentation and project note, the shortcut cannot be confirmed as compliant, so the proposed work fails and must not proceed.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "NEC 310",
    "NEC Table 310.16"
  ]
}
